key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape memory functionality, but only after annealing to overcome as-sprayed microstructural defects.
engineering_relevance: This offers a promising route to functional metallic coatings for wear, friction, corrosion, and thermal protection where active actuation or self-adaptive behavior is desired.
method: HVAF sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Characterized chemical composition, porosity, microstructure, and phase transformation
Tested functional response with 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch experiments
evidence_signal: The coatings were reported to be 100–300 µm thick and, despite porosity, oxides, voids, and dislocations, still showed martensitic transformation and thermomechanical actuation after annealing.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous and mechanically limited, so post-spray annealing and adhesion/strength control are critical for practical use.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
